lede: A look at February 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ona Huczkowski and Dimas Delgado, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2023 Turkey–Syria earthquakes
events.desc: Two earthquakes measuring Mww 7.8 and 7.5 struck near the border between Turkey and Syria with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XII (Extreme). The earthquakes resulted in numerous aftershocks and a death toll of 57,658 people.

events.title: Willamette Valley flood of 1996
events.desc: Willamette Valley Flood: Floods in the Willamette Valley of Oregon, United States, causes over US$500 million in property damage throughout the Pacific Northwest.

events.title: Mary Gaudron
events.desc: Justice Mary Gaudron becomes the first woman to be appointed to the High Court of Australia.

events.title: Cape Canaveral
events.desc: At Cape Canaveral, Florida, the first successful test firing of a Titan intercontinental ballistic missile is accomplished.

events.title: Manchester United F.C.
events.desc: Eight Manchester United F.C. players and 15 other passengers are killed in the Munich air disaster.

events.title: Washington Naval Treaty
events.desc: The Washington Naval Treaty is signed in Washington, D.C., limiting the naval armaments of United States, Britain, Japan, France, and Italy.

events.title: Permanent Court of Arbitration
events.desc: The Permanent Court of Arbitration, an international arbitration court at The Hague, is created when the Senate of the Netherlands ratifies an 1899 peace conference decree.

events.title: Municipalities of Finland
events.desc: The municipal administration of Finland is established.

events.title: 1819 Singapore Treaty
events.desc: The Treaty of Singapore was signed by Sir Thomas Stamford Raffles, Hussein Shah of Johor, and Temenggong Abdul Rahman, and it is now recognised as the founding of modern Singapore.

events.title: Massachusetts
events.desc: Massachusetts becomes the sixth state to ratify the United States Constitution.

births.name: Robert Maillart
births.line: Swiss engineer, designed the Salginatobel Bridge and Schwandbach Bridge (died 1940)

births.name: John Keane, 1st Baron Keane
births.line: Irish general and politician, Governor of Saint Lucia (died 1844)

deaths.text: James Hadley Chase, English-Swiss soldier and author
Jaan Soots, Estonian general and politician, 7th Estonian Minister of War

observances.text: Christian feast day: Mateo Correa Magallanes (one of Saints of the Cristero War)
Christian feast day: Paul Miki and Twenty-six Martyrs of Japan
